title: Ability to set custom quarters/ annual views
excerpt: We roadmap our development work based on our financial quarters, which are offset one month from calendar quarters (so our Q1 2021 runs Feb-April). We would love the ability to set custom quarters in the timeline view to…

posts.author: hscherer (Hilary Scherer)
posts.cookedHtml: We roadmap our development work based on our financial quarters, which are offset one month from calendar quarters (so our Q1 2021 runs Feb-April). We would love the ability to set custom quarters in the timeline view to minimize the amount of translation needed between calendar <> financial quarters.

posts.author: SarahJacobs (Sarah Jacobs)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, I’ve love to know how to do this too as I want to set quarterly deadlines

posts.author: Zoe (Zoe)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree here. In Australia our financial year is July-June and we report as a company on these quarters, so it’s very confusing to have different quarters in Monday.

posts.author: jenlpro (Jen Provenzano)
posts.cookedHtml: Here to vote for this functionality as well! Really disappointed that it doesn’t already exist, as SO many companies (especially SaaS) have moved their fiscal years to Feb - Jan

posts.author: StephaniePegg (Stephanie Pegg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that it would be helpful to be able to set these to fit our financial year (for my company, Quarter 1 is Jul-Sep).

Thanks

posts.author: m.alegria (Manuel Alegria)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature since my company’s fiscal year starts in October. Since it only support calendar quarters, my workaround is to re-create the gantt chart in another tool for presentations or take a screenshot of the Monday.com chart and photoshop the correct fiscal quarters. Both workaround require more work than is needed. Please consider adding this enhancement request to your roadmap.

posts.author: halby (Halby V)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a must have basic feature to have on any work management tool.

We recently moved to Monday.com and our FY is from Sep-Aug, our roadmap is pointless without custom quarter aligning without FY. We can’t use Monday now as this was one of our primary use cases to choose and never see this coming.

posts.author: SimAujla (Sim Aujla)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Support,

I’m from New Zealand and our business requires us to report using the New Zealand Financial Year (April-March) quarters.

I hope the developers can come with a solution soon as this was first discussed in April 2021 and we are sitting near the end of 2023 with no updates of the ability to set custom quarters.

posts.author: gsnetx-techsupport (techsupport)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ll jump on this request as well. Our FY starts on 10/1.

posts.author: woodwarper (Jason Michael Culp)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this functionality. It’s embarrassing to show a timeline to anyone in my leadership team with the quarters off. It causes so much confusion in meetings that could be avoided.

posts.author: jergood (Joel Ergood)
posts.cookedHtml: Just to keep this feature request alive… we’d like it as well FY starts March.

posts.author: BeccaM (Becca Matthews)
posts.cookedHtml: This is desperately needed. You can set when the week starts so you should definitely be able to set when the year starts.

posts.author: martinhennig (Martin Hennig)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love to see calendar views to support displaying custom timeframes vs. only day week and month. Maybe quarterly or yearly views? Use case would e.g. be to see all events happening in 2020 vs. just immediately upcoming ones. A current workaround may be to put custom dates in search everything, which is far from ideal. Are there better ways?

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: We achieve what you are looking for using status columns. Create a column for each quarter, month, etc and filter for it in a calendar view or dashboard. Another option is to separate the events by group (we use automations to do this). Top group, upcoming (maybe this quarter), etc and filter your view.

posts.author: martinhennig (Martin Hennig)
posts.cookedHtml:

Great, thanks! We’ll try that

posts.cookedHtml:
abiciea:

g for using status columns. Create a column for each quarter, month, etc and filter for it in a calendar

Hi, did you manage to sort this out? I have the same question as you. I tried as abiciea suggested, but it didnt work out.

posts.author: percypig (Philip Percival)
posts.cookedHtml: We are an events based company and would very much benefit from a yearly view calendar option - essentially like a yearly wall planner. Ideally, this could be viewed in horizontal or vertical formats, and with the ability to show multiple Google Calendars - the way we already can as if we were using the GCal app.

posts.author: RASCHOON (RS)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know that I am bringing back an old topic/feature request, but I honestly cannot believe that this doesn’t exist yet. I’m trying to do a simple product release calendar and I want to view things to be released by quarter.

I’ve tried a Kanban view where I have a status column for each QYYYY, which works, but is manual as they have to fill in the actual release date and also a status quarter.

I’ve created a formula column that changes the release date to the quarter, works great, but you can’t use that for much in any other view (would love to be able to use that in a Kanban view).

Best I’ve come up with is a timeline and card view, but it’s not very nice to look at…

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @martinhennig @abiciea @percypig @Domyx @RASCHOON , We’ve developed a new calendar app that enables you to transform items into dynamic calendar events with daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly views. check it out: monday.com apps marketplace

posts.author: MCanterll (Mike Cantrell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday Community,

I have several projects that are either in queue that have TBD dates or projected start dates that are defined in either Quaters or Months. Is there a way to add a feature where you can select a month or quarter for the start date. Of course we narrow this down, but this would be helpful when looking at the queue and forecasting. This would also be helpful when breaking projects down in the Kanban Board without adding several columns.

Unless this is already a feature and if so can someone guide me on how this would work?

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MCanterll!

Have you considered adding status columns with values for each month/Quarter? This would allow you to categorize the projects by month and you could later set a different column start date when you plan the actual date.

You could then use either a kenban, or chart or dashboard the projects schedule for each month/quarter.
The other option is you could set the date column to the first day of the quarter or month you are scheduling and then create a chart with date as the x axis and grouped by month/Quarter.

Hopefully I’m on the right track with your question, let me know if that helps or if you have additional Qs.

posts.author: MelB (Melanie Broxis)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like the ability to show Dates as months or quarters also. I don’t really want to have to select it from a drop down, I just want to be able to select a due date then another date column will automatically show which quarter it is due in. Is this possible? Thanks!

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Take a look here:

Dates as months or quarters Feature requests
I would like the ability to show Dates as months or quarters. Ideally I would like to be able to select a due date then another date column will automatically show which quarter it is due in.

You might find what you need.

posts.author: annadayton (Anna Dayton)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice to be able to filter if the date was in “the last 2 quarters” as an option or even better if the date is “in the current or last quarter”

posts.author: MelB (Melanie Broxis)

I would like the ability to show Dates as months or quarters.
Ideally I would like to be able to select a due date then another date column will automatically show which quarter it is due in.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@MelB

It would be great if there was better control over how dates and numbers were formatted.

You do have more control when using a formula column. You might find these formulas helpful.

"Q" & FORMAT_DATE({Date}, "Q-YYYY")

FORMAT_DATE({Date}, "M-YYYY")

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
